title: Chest Pain — Cardiac History
description: 47-year-old female with acute chest pain, prior MI, on warfarin and metoprolol — aspirin given by husband pre-arrival

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Hi there, I'm a paramedic. What's going on today?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: I have chest pain. It started about two hours ago.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Okay, can you tell me your name and date of birth?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: Sarah Chen. March 12th, 1978.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Thanks, Sarah. Can you describe the pain — where is it, and what does it feel like?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: It's sharp, on the left side of my chest. It's about a seven out of ten.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Does it go anywhere else — your arm, jaw, back?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: A little bit down my left arm.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Any shortness of breath, sweating, or nausea?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: Yes, I've been sweating and I feel a bit nauseous.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Do you have any medical history — any past heart problems?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: Yes, I had a heart attack two years ago. I also have high blood pressure and type 2 diabetes.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: What medications are you on?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: Metoprolol and warfarin. I take them every day.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Any allergies to medications?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: No, none that I know of.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Did you take anything for the pain before we arrived?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: My husband gave me an aspirin about twenty minutes ago.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Good. Any idea how much you weigh?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: About 68 kilograms.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: I'm going to listen to your chest and check you over now.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Breath sounds are equal bilaterally. Skin is pale and diaphoretic. Heart rate 102, blood pressure 148 over 94.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_2
utterances.text: Twelve-lead is printing now.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Sarah, we're going to start an IV and put you on some oxygen. We'll get you to the hospital quickly.

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: Is it my heart again?

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: We're not sure yet, but we're treating this seriously. The ECG will help us understand what's happening. You're in good hands.
